instant.$description: No animation. Required fallback for prefers-reduced-motion.

fast.$description: Micro interactions. Hover highlights, focus rings, active press states.

normal.$description: Default transition. Buttons, toggles, checkboxes, switches.

moderate.$description: Small element entry. Dropdowns opening, tooltips appearing, badges.

slow.$description: Medium element entry. Modals, side panels, expanding sections.

slower.$description: Large element entry. Bottom sheets, page-level transitions.

slowest.$description: Deliberate, expressive. Onboarding steps, splash screens, feature highlights.

linear.$description: Constant velocity. Progress bars, loading spinners, looping animations.

ease-in.$description: Accelerates into exit. Use for elements LEAVING the screen (closing, dismissing, fading out).

ease-out.$description: Decelerates into rest. Use for elements ENTERING the screen (appearing, sliding in, expanding).

ease-in-out.$description: Default for most UI transitions. Elements that move from one position to another within the screen.

spring.$description: Slight overshoot, then settles. Modals appearing, bottom sheets, playful confirmations. Use sparingly.

sharp.$description: Quick snap with abrupt end. Menus, tooltips, snappy feedback states. Feels responsive, not floaty.

none.$description: No delay. Default for all transitions.

short.$description: Slight stagger. Sequential list items, staggered card reveals.

medium.$description: Noticeable pause. Step-by-step onboarding, tooltip after hover intent.

long.$description: Deliberate delay. Confirmation feedback, loading state entry.

skeleton.$description: Skeleton screen delay — wait before showing loading state to avoid flash on fast loads.
